description: Per-beach minefield defaults ported from TIV defaults/beaches.json Minefield blocks (stripped from data/beaches.json in D1-A, recovered for D3 anti-ship/mine warfare). 'geometry' + 'transit' configure the geometric danger model (port of TaiwanDefenseRefactor/mine_warfare.py): mines are scattered in a length x width field, the fleet takes a randomized straight approach path, and only mines within danger_radius of that path are 'dangerous'. The goal is to clear a LANE, not the whole field.

transit._comment: prelanding_clear_per_sweeper: dangerous mines a single assigned minesweeper clears pre-landing (closest first). Default 1 => with the ~1-2 sweepers a beach gets, pre-landing clearing mainly LOCATES the field (clears 1-2), it does not open the lane. The transiting fleet (decoys first, then ascending value) detonates the rest; a decoy that survives a detonation continues and can trigger subsequent mines. Amphibs are only at risk if dangerous mines remain after decoys + sweeping.
